description: This figure presents S-N (stress vs. number of cycles) fatigue curves for Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic steel under three test conditions. The x-axis denotes the number of cycles N in units of 10^6, and the y-axis denotes cyclic stress +/- σ in MPa. Curve 1 (diamond markers) corresponds to fatigue tests at 293 K, curve 2 (circle markers) corresponds to tests at 673 K, and curve 3 (triangle markers) corresponds to thermocycling tests alternating between 293 K and 673 K. All three curves show a decreasing trend of tolerable stress as cycle number increases. The 673 K test yields the highest fatigue strength across all cycle counts, while the 293 K test yields the lowest; the thermocycling curve falls between these two, indicating thermocycling fatigue performance is intermediate between constant low and high temperature conditions. The observed higher fatigue strength at elevated temperature is attributed to time-dependent deformation aging strengthening in microplastic deformation zones, supporting the conclusion that Fe-Mn-Al-C steels are suitable for service under cyclic loading combined with periodic temperature changes.
